content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN, an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N) is called a prefix maxima if and only if:

Ai‚â•Aj for every j‚â§iA_i \ge A_j \text{ for every } j \le iAi‚Äã‚â•Aj‚Äã for every j‚â§iWe define a function PMXPMXPMX as follows:


Let PPP be the set of all prefix maximas of AAA.
Then, PMX(A)=‚®ÅX‚ààPAX\text{PMX}(A) = \bigoplus_{X \in P} A_XPMX(A)=‚®ÅX‚ààP‚ÄãAX‚Äã
where ‚®Å\bigoplus‚®Å denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

That is, PMX(A)PMX(A)PMX(A) denotes the bitwise XOR of all the prefix maxima of AAA.

Two arrays AAA and BBB of length NNN are said to be distinct if there exists at least one index iii  (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N) such that Ai‚âÝBiA_i \neq B_iAi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Bi‚Äã.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Let SSS be the set of all rearrangements of AAA, without duplicates.

For example, if A=[1,1,2]A = [1, 1, 2]A=[1,1,2], then S={[1,1,2],[1,2,1],[2,1,1]}S = \{[1, 1, 2], [1, 2, 1], [2, 1, 1]\}S={[1,1,2],[1,2,1],[2,1,1]}.

Your task is to compute the expected value of PMX(B)PMX(B)PMX(B) across all arrays B‚ààSB\in SB‚ààS.

Output the result modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353‚ÄÝ998\,244\,353^\dagger998244353‚ÄÝ.

‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ Formally, let M=998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353M = 998\,244\,353M=998244353. It can be shown that the answer can be expressed as an irreducible fraction pq\frac{p}{q}qp‚Äã, where ppp and qqq are integers and q‚â°Ã∏0(modM)q \not \equiv 0 \pmod{M}qÓÄÝ‚â°0(modM). Output the integer equal to p‚ãÖq‚àí1‚Äämod‚ÄäMp \cdot q^{-1} \bmod Mp‚ãÖq‚àí1modM. In other words, output such an integer xxx that 0‚â§x<M0 \le x < M0‚â§x<M and x‚ãÖq‚â°p(modM)x \cdot q \equiv p \pmod{M}x‚ãÖq‚â°p(modM).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the size of array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the expected value of PMX(B)PMX(B)PMX(B) across all distinct rearrangements of AAA, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For A=[1,1,1]A = [1, 1, 1]A=[1,1,1], the only rearrangement is AAA itself.
We have PMX([1,1,1])=1‚äï1‚äï1=1PMX([1, 1, 1]) = 1\oplus 1\oplus 1 = 1PMX([1,1,1])=1‚äï1‚äï1=1, so the answer is 111.
Test case 222: For A=[1,2]A = [1, 2]A=[1,2], we have the following rearrangements:

[1,2][1, 2][1,2], for which PMX([1,2])=1‚äï2=3PMX([1, 2]) = 1\oplus 2 = 3PMX([1,2])=1‚äï2=3.
[2,1][2, 1][2,1], for which PMX([2,1])=2PMX([2, 1]) = 2PMX([2,1])=2.

The expected value is hence
12‚ãÖ(3+2)=52\frac{1}{2} \cdot (3 + 2) = \frac{5}{2}21‚Äã‚ãÖ(3+2)=25‚Äã
It can be verified that this is 2‚ãÖ499122179‚â°5(mod998244353)2\cdot 499122179 \equiv 5 \pmod {998244353}2‚ãÖ499122179‚â°5(mod998244353).
